channel.title: Texts on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in Texts on emsenn.net

item.title: Compute Infrastructure for Small Organizations
item.description:
A small organization building a software system faces an infrastructure question that larger organizations can defer to specialized teams: where does the system run? The answer determines cost, operational burden, scaling path, and the degree of control the organization retains over its own systems. This text surveys the real options as of 2026, with documented pricing and practitioner-reported tradeoffs.

Virtual private servers

A virtual private server (VPS) is a virtual machine rented from a hosting provider, accessed via SSH, with full root control. It is the most common starting point for small teams.

item.title: Remote Development Environments
item.description:
A remote development environment is any arrangement where the developer’s editor runs locally but code execution, file storage, and tooling run on a separate machine. The practice is older than the term — Unix terminals connected to shared servers in the 1970s were remote development environments — but the modern form emerged around 2019 with VS Code Remote SSH and has since split into three distinct architectures.

SSH-based remote development

The simplest architecture: a developer connects to a remote machine over SSH, and the editor handles the split transparently. The remote machine runs the language server, file system, terminal, and build tools. The local machine handles rendering and input.
